page: Microsoft Azure E20s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Esv6 server family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, featuring 10 physical cores and 20 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The system is equipped with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for accelerated matrix operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the E20s_v6 demonstrates strong performance in single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression at 31.47 GB/sec and Geekbench single-core testing. It also achieves top-tier performance in LLM text generation for small 135M models, reaching 408.39 tokens per second. However, multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth tests fall within the average range. A notable hardware tradeoff is the total absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, requiring reliance on external Premium Storage for data persistence.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E20s_v6 offers a high-density memory configuration relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of paying for excess compute cores. This resource profile is highly suitable for relational database management systems, in-memory caching layers like Redis, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: The Microsoft Azure E20s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, configured with 20 vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, it features 10 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core performance, particularly in gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. Tradeoffs include the absence of local storage and dedicated GPUs, necessitating remote Premium Storage. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads, making it well-suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM featuring 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, ideal for databases and caching.
tagline: An Intel-powered memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory density and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise and database workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 20 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
160.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Strong single-core benchmark results in gzip compression and text processing
No local storage or GPU, requiring remote Premium Storage configurations
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and enterprise applications
